name: Stir Fried Clams in Chile Garlic Black Bean S
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp Chinese salted and fermented black beans
1 Inch lump fresh ginger, peeled and minced
2 to 3 fresh green chiles, sliced diagonally
2 tbsp Chinese Shaoxing rice wine or dry vermouth
1 tsp Cornstarch mixed with 1 tablespoon water
recipeInstructions:
1. Scrub clams and rinse well with cold water. Discard any that do not shut quickly or have broken shells. Keep refrigerated in a bowl covered with a damp towel until ready to cook.
2. Soak the black beans in lukewarm water to cover for 5 minutes. Rinse with fresh water and transfer to a small bowl with the ginger and garlic. Using the back of a fork, gently mash the mixture into a coarse paste; set aside.
3. Place a wok over high heat. When hot, add the oil, then the black bean paste, chiles, and green onions. Stir rapidly until fragrant, about 15 seconds.
4. Add clams and stir-fry for 1 minute. Season with sugar and white pepper. Splash in the rice wine and toss together for a few seconds.
5. Add soy sauce and chicken stock, cover, reduce heat to medium-high, and simmer for 3 to 5 minutes, or until clams open.
6. Pour cornstarch mixture into the center of the wok. Stir continuously until sauce thickens to a creamy glaze. Drizzle in the sesame oil.
7. Serve with steamed rice.
Serves 6 with other dishes.
